t_ch: 哦吼？岂不知多少悖论都是由数学家发现的？
t_en: Oh-ho? I wonder how many paradoxes were discovered by mathematicians?

t_ch: 更何况，这城中擅长玩弄悖论的何止是本狮？都是在广场、街头和法庭里学来的吼！
t_en: Moreover, I am not the only one adept at paradoxical plays in this city. I learned it from the plaza, streets, and justice court!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 爱好幽默的狮子…你决定再尝试一次，看看它还会喜欢什么样的答案。
t_en: A lion that loves jokes... You decide to try one more time. Let's see what other answers it likes.

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 你不想再花时间研究一头狮子的喜好了，因为这听起来真的非常奇怪，不是吗？
t_en: You don't want to waste any more time figuring out a lion's inclinations. Why? Because it's super weird, that's why.

t_ch: 瞧瞧，是谁来拜访本狮了，是异乡的勇士啊，又想要接受智慧的考验了么？
t_en: Look who's come to visit this lion, a brave outsider. Back for more brain-teasers?

t_ch: 吼吼，听好了，问题很简单：<color=#dbc291ff>「什么东西只能把他人打倒，自己却永远站不起来？」</color>
t_en: Ho-ho, listen well. This one's an easy one: <color=#dbc291ff>"What can only be knocked down by others, but can never stand up on its own?"</color>

t_ch: 嘿嘿，切莫急着回答，还有一个要求——
t_en: Heehee, don't be hasty with your answer. I have another request...

t_ch: 寻常的答案，本狮可不喜欢。我要的是有意想不到的幽默感、能令狮子我捧腹大笑的答案，记住这一点吼！
t_en: I dislike regular answers. I want funny ones that will surprise me and send me into bursts of laughter. Remember this well!

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 问对人了！幽默感…你从不缺少这个东西。无论是把在场听众都整沉默的冷笑话，还是各种谐音梗，你全都信手拈来。
t_en: That's totally you! A sense of humor... You've never lacked in this department. Be it the dad jokes that silence the audience, or all sorts of puns, you know 'em all like the back of your hand.

t_ch: 谜底就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 这根球棒！
t_en: This ball bat!

t_ch: 正是银河球棒侠的武器！打倒过的对手不计其数，但…谁会有办法让球棒站起来？恐怕连那位以欺诈与鬼把戏闻名的{RUBY_B#扎格列斯}神{RUBY_E#}也做不到。
t_en: It's none other than the Galactic Baseballer's weapon itself! It has beaten down countless opponents, but... who can let a ball bat stand on its own? Even {RUBY_B#Zagreus}the Titan{RUBY_E#} renowned for their cunning and trickery would be hard-pressed to achieve this.

t_ch: 吼吼，有点幽默，但不太多。回答得太过宽泛，如此说来，尼卡多利的长矛岂非也是答案？
t_en: Ho-ho, it's a little funny, but not by much. And the answer's too general. If that's the case, wouldn't Nikador's lance also be the answer?

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么还会咬人的…你只是想抖个机灵而已……
t_en: OUCH! This lion bit you... It was just a wisecrack...

t_ch: 真言狮口。
t_en: Verax Leo.

t_ch: 没错，答案就是眼前的这头狮子，你已经学会了以子之矛攻子之盾的答法。
t_en: That's right, the answer is the lion before you. You've learned to turn the tables on its answers.

t_ch: 喜欢用古怪的谜语刁难答题人，热衷于在智慧的层面打倒别人。不过，没有四肢和脚爪的狮子，大概是不能*站*的。
t_en: Likes to stump the answerer with bizarre riddles, eager to outsmart others in a battle of wits. But a lion without limbs and paws probably can't stand.

t_ch: 哼哼，虽然推论的过程没有毛病，但这个答案真是令本狮笑不出来。你，讲话太直白了吼！
t_en: Heh-heh, your logic is sound, but this answer does not make me laugh. Your words are too candid!

t_ch: 它急了！虽然你输了问答，但总觉得——这把是你赢了！
t_en: It's angry! You've got the wrong answer, but you feel like you've won this round!

t_ch: 沐浴香皂。
t_en: Bath soap.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的浴场里常见的沐浴香皂，掉在地上像烂泥一样滑溜，踩到它的人只有跌倒的份。
t_en: Bath soap is a staple in Okhema's baths. It could also slide all around like sloppy mud when dropped on the floor. The unfortunate soul who slips on it can only fall.

t_ch: 同时，你非常确信，「烂泥」是绝对扶不上墙的。
t_en: At the same time, you're very sure that sloppy mud can't stick to walls.

t_ch: 哦吼？有趣，有趣，看来你不是个倒霉蛋，就是个恶作剧高手。无论是哪一种，都让本狮非常愉悦啊，哈哈哈……
t_en: Oh-ho? Interesting, interesting. You're either an unlucky fool or a master prankster. Either way, I am highly amused, hahaha...

t_ch: 算你答对了，趁本狮笑得合不拢嘴的功夫，快把手拿出去吧！
t_en: I'll consider it the right answer. While I am laughing with my jaw wide open, you should quickly take your hand out!

t_ch: 呜呜，我没有幽默感。
t_en: Boohoo, I am a humorless cretin.

t_ch: 好吧，最天才的喜剧大师也有词穷的时刻，你的幽默感已经山穷水尽，再想下去只会有烂梗，还是放过自己吧。
t_en: All right, even the greatest comedic geniuses will sometimes be at a loss for words. Your sense of humor has already been exhausted — all you have will be terrible jokes. You should just give yourself a break.

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 你确信奥赫玛的朋友们当中有比你更懂幽默的人，邀请谁来回答这个问题好呢？
t_en: Are you sure that you have friends in Okhema who have a better sense of humor than you? Who will you invite to answer this riddle?

t_ch: 问问遐蝶吧。
t_en: Let's ask Castorice.

t_ch: …原来我在{NICKNAME}眼中是有幽默感的人吗？
t_en: ...So, {NICKNAME}, you think I'm funny?

t_ch: 当然，我相信你。
t_en: Of course, I believe in you.

t_ch: 不，我只是想看你被咬。
t_en: No, I just want to see you get bitten.

t_ch: 虽然不知道你为什么想看…但我也很好奇这种感觉是什么。
t_en: I don't know why you'd want to see that... but I'm also curious how it feels.

t_ch: 这个问题让我有些苦恼呢，答案…也许是「歪曲的逻辑」吧。
t_en: This question vexes me somewhat. The answer... is probably "twisted logic."

t_ch: 记得在树庭求学时，我听过几节精彩的辩论。假如逻辑分为正直的逻辑和歪曲的逻辑，后者往往能用诡计把前者驳倒，但歪曲的逻辑本身却是不成立的。
t_en: I remember that I listened to a few outstanding debate sessions when I was studying at the Grove. Hypothetically speaking, if logic is divided into sound and twisted, the latter often employs trickery to refute the former, but twisted logic is fundamentally flawed.

t_ch: 抱歉…想到的只有这些，希望能够帮到你。
t_en: Sorry... That's all I can think of. I hope it helps.

t_ch: 同意，是「歪曲的逻辑」。
t_en: I agree. It's "twisted logic."

t_ch: 哈——欠，真是无懈可击的回答，完全符合正直的逻辑。可惜可惜，这个回答太过板正，幽默感有些不足呐！
t_en: Yaaawn. A flawless answer, completely fitting of sound logic. Alas, this answer is too middling. It lacks humor!

t_ch: 那个…请把手拿出去吧，我听过你的故事，狮狮还不想死。
t_en: Then... please take your hand out. I've heard about you, and I don't want to die yet.

t_ch: 遐蝶向你和狮口行礼，有些遗憾地离开了……
t_en: Castorice bows to you and the lion, and departs reluctantly...

t_ch: 似乎不太对。
t_en: That doesn't sound right.

t_ch: 「歪曲的逻辑」…这个回答真的足够幽默吗？再问问其他人的看法吧。
t_en: Twisted logic... Is this answer humorous enough? Let's get a second opinion.

t_ch: 问问白厄吧。
t_en: Let's ask Phainon.

t_ch: 从字面意义上说，我猜是{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Literally speaking, I'm guessing it is {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 因为要背负黎明机器，所以*站*不起来…嗯？这个笑话没有太冷吧？
t_en: Because it has to bear the Dawn Device, so it can't stand up... Hmm? Is this joke not deadpan enough?

t_ch: 妙啊！谜底是刻法勒！
t_en: Excellent! The answer is Kephale!

t_ch: …好冷，好冷，冷得我的鬃毛都要一根根竖起来了！
t_en: ...It's so, so bad, it's making the hair stand on their ends!

t_ch: 趁本狮冻僵了的功夫，快把手拿出去吧！
t_en: While I'm recovering from that bad joke, hurry and take your hand away!

t_ch: 冒犯刻法勒的笑话居然还是冷笑话，幽默可真是门学问……
t_en: To think the joke that offends Kephale is deadpan humor, humor is certainly an art...

t_ch: 似乎不太对。
t_en: That doesn't sound right.

t_ch: 听起来是挺幽默的，但用刻法勒开玩笑，你总感觉自己似乎犯了渎神的罪过。再问问其他人的看法吧。
t_en: It's pretty funny, but you feel like you've committed the sin of blasphemy by making Kephale the butt of the joke. Let's ask what someone else thinks.

t_ch: 或许，问问阿格莱雅？
t_en: Or maybe, let's ask Aglaea?

t_ch: 你有点担心阿格莱雅不喜欢无意义的玩笑，不过，通讯里她的声音听起来并不反感，甚至…饶有兴味。
t_en: You're a little worried that Aglaea might dislike meaningless jokes, but her voice over the comms doesn't sound too perturbed. She even sounds... amused.

t_ch: 元老院。
t_en: The Council of Elders.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅的答案如此简单而笃定，你不禁细想起了背后的深意…元老院能靠权力打倒别人，但站不起来是指…算了，这种笑话想得太细就不好笑了。
t_en: Aglaea's answer is simple, yet determined. You can't help but contemplate the deeper meaning behind it... The Council of Elders crushes others with its authority, but it can't stand because... Forget it, jokes stop being funny when you dissect them.

t_ch: 没错，是元老院。
t_en: That's right, it's the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 哦吼？纵然本狮不便亲口妄议奥赫玛的政务，却非常乐于听到影射此事的笑话呐！
t_en: Oh-ho? It is inconvenient for me to speak ill of Okhema's political affairs, but I am very happy to listen to jokes that allude to such matters!

t_ch: 妙答！妙答！本狮认可你的幽默感了，快快快——把手拿出去吧！
t_en: What an answer! Amazing! I acknowledge your sense of humor! Quickly, take your hand away!

t_ch: 似乎不太对。
t_en: That doesn't sound right.

t_ch: 身为外邦人，擅自议论奥赫玛的管理事务恐怕有些不妥，还是再问问其他人的看法吧。
t_en: As an outsider, it may not be appropriate to be discussing Okhema's administrative affairs. Better ask for someone else's opinion.

t_ch: 还是相信自己吧！
t_en: It's better to believe in yourself!

t_ch: 吼吼吼！问答时间结束，这道题的答案是——没有标准答案。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! You're out of time. The answer to this question is that there is no standard answer.

t_ch: 很疑惑吗？幽默怎么会有固定的形状，一旦想要裁定标准，就失去了让人会心一笑的魅力所在了吼！
t_en: Confused? How could humor have a fixed form? Once you impose a standard on such a concept, it will lose all the charm that causes spontaneous laughter!

t_ch: 本狮和城中的其他同类不同，无论是什么答案，只要能让本狮发笑，都是正确答案。要想抓住本狮的笑点，不妨再试一次。
t_en: I am different from my brethren in the city. No matter what the answer is, as long as you make me laugh, they are all correct answers. You can make a few more attempts to see what tickles my fancy.

t_ch: 本狮和城中的其他同类不同，无论是什么答案，只要能让本狮发笑，都是正确答案。听到了两次有趣的回答，本狮已经心满意足了吼！
t_en: I am different from my other brethren in the city. No matter what the answer is, as long as you make me laugh, they are all correct answers. I will be satisfied when I hear two interesting answers!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼吼，听好了，我要问的问题是：<color=#dbc291ff>「什么东西无论在自己手中，还是在他人手中，这两个人都是一无所有？」</color>
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, listen well, my question is: <color=#dbc291ff>"What results in two people being left with nothing, whether it is in the hands of either person?"</color>

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 「一无所有」，这个词并不陌生，毕竟在空间站醒来的时候，你就是一无所有。想想看，什么东西会让你的心情接近那一刻？
t_en: Left with nothing. This phrase is not alien to you. When you awoke in the space station, that was what you were. Think hard, what is the thing that is closest to the feeling you had back then?

t_ch: 谜底就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 零分考卷。
t_en: An exam paper with zero points.

t_ch: 「零分！下一个！」——熟悉的声音在你的脑海里响起。毫无疑问，拥有了却等同于一无所有的东西，是一张被打了零分的考卷。
t_en: "Zero Points!" A familiar voice echoes in your mind. Undoubtedly, an exam paper with zero points is something that you own that equals to nothing.

t_ch: 吼吼吼，真的吗？拿到零分考卷的人，虽然没有奖励，却说不定有惩罚在等着，怎么能算一无所有呢？瞧瞧，本狮的惩罚这就要开始了——
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, is that so? While a person who scored zero on an exam gets no rewards, there may be punishment awaiting them. How is that nothing? Let's see, this lion's punishment is about to commence...

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子居然搞体罚，真是个坏老师……
t_en: Ouch! This lion's dishing out physical punishment. What a bad teacher...

t_ch: 哲学思考。
t_en: Philosophical thinking.

t_ch: 没错，你在书上看到过，知识的效用就在于消除偏见——「我知道的，就是我一无所知」。这才是真正的一无所有。
t_en: That's right. You saw that in a book: The utility of knowledge is to expunge prejudice — "All I know is that I know nothing." This is truly being left with nothing.

t_ch: 哦吼？异乡的勇士，难道也向奥赫玛的哲学家学来了辩论的思维？可惜可惜，至少你还拥有一口袋的妙论，这不是正确答案。
t_en: Oh-ho? Brave outsider, could you have picked the brains of Okhema's philosophers? Alas, though you have a pocketful of clever rhetoric, this is a wrong answer.

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子居然搞体罚，真是个坏老师……
t_en: Ouch! This lion's dishing out physical punishment. What a bad teacher...

t_ch: 强化方向错误的道具。
t_en: A piece of gear that enhances all the wrong sub stats.

t_ch: 还有什么比朝错误方向强化的道具更「一无所有」的存在呢？投入所有资源，最终得到的东西却并不是你需要的，这种痛你绝对不想经历第二次。
t_en: What concept could be more "nothing" than something that ardently strengthens you in the wrong direction? Investing all your resources, only to end up with something that you don't need — You will never want to experience this pain a second time.

t_ch: 吼吼，很沉痛的回答，见者伤心，闻者落泪呐！可惜可惜，这样的道具只能放在自己的背包里，不能送给别人，这不是正确答案。
t_en: Ho-ho, a poignant answer. Those who see it are saddened, and those who hear it are tearing up! Alas, such an artifact should only be left in your own bag, never to be gifted to someone else. This is not the right answer.

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子居然搞体罚，真是个坏老师……
t_en: Ouch! This lion's dishing out physical punishment. What a bad teacher...

t_ch: 想不出来了…
t_en: I can't think of anything...

t_ch: 使用传信石板求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 求己不如求人，要选择奥赫玛的哪位朋友来替你回答这个刁钻的问题呢？
t_en: There's no shame in seeking help from others. Which friend from Okhema would you like to seek help from for answering this tricky question?

t_ch: 问问缇宝她们？
t_en: How about asking Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne?

t_ch: 信息发出去了，孩子们回复的速度一如既往地快。
t_en: The message is sent, and they reply in their usual speedy manner.

t_ch: 是什么呢？是寄丢的东西吗？
t_en: What could it be? A lost package?

t_ch: 唔…只要是交到*我们*手里的东西，无论怎样都会送到收信人那里。
t_en: Mmm... as long as it's something left in our hands, we'll always deliver it to the receiver's hands.

t_ch: 除非是特别粗心的信使，把东西弄丢了。这样的话，收信人和寄信人两边都是两手空空、一无所有啦！
t_en: Unless it's a very absent-minded messenger who lost the parcel. If that's the case, the sender, and receiver will both be empty-handed, left with nothing!

t_ch: 是粗心的信使弄丢的东西。
t_en: It is a parcel lost by a careless messenger.

t_ch: 吼吼，信使弄丢的东西，根本还没来得及送到收信人手里吧？怎么能算作「在他人手中」？可惜可惜，这不是正确答案。
t_en: Ho-ho, a parcel lost by a messenger never made it into the receiver's hands in the first place! How can that be considered "in their hands"? Alas, this is a wrong answer.

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子居然搞体罚，真是个坏老师……
t_en: Ouch! This lion's dishing out physical punishment. What a bad teacher...

t_ch: 再想想？
t_en: Think again?

t_ch: 缇宝她们的答案暂且记作参考，再问问其他人的想法吧。
t_en: Let's take the girls' answer into consideration, and seek another's opinion in the meantime.

t_ch: 试试问下遐蝶。
t_en: Try asking Castorice.

t_ch: …以我所见过的事情来说，是「仇恨」吧。
t_en: ...From what I have experienced, it is "hatred."

t_ch: 在「纷争世」的战场上，我曾目睹许多惨烈的画面，无数战争的起因都是城邦与城邦、人与人之间的恨意和复仇。
t_en: In the battlefields of Era Bellica, I once witnessed many tragic scenes, and countless wars were caused by hatred and vengeance between cities and people.

t_ch: 手握仇恨不肯放下，最后的结果便是…战争让双方都变得一无所有。
t_en: Refusing to let go of hatred in their hands would only result in... a war that leaves both parties with nothing.

t_ch: 没错，是无法放下的仇恨。
t_en: That's right. It's hatred that people refuse to let go.

t_ch: 哦吼？「仇恨」，有意思的角度，尽管不是本狮心中所想的那一样，但却毫不逊色于原本的答案呐——
t_en: Oh-ho? Hatred? An interesting perspective. Even if it's not what I was thinking, it rivals the original answer...

t_ch: 冤冤相报何时了，不错，本狮决定放过你的手，这不过是一次「友好」的问答交流，吼吼吼。
t_en: Vengeance only begets more vengeance. Fine, I have decided to free your hand. This is but a friendly exchange of answers, ho-ho-ho.

t_ch: 再想想？
t_en: Think again?

t_ch: 总觉得这头狮子更喜欢剑走偏锋的答案，再问问其他人的想法吧。
t_en: You feel that this lion prefers answers off the beaten path. Let's ask someone else's thoughts.

t_ch: 万敌会怎么说？
t_en: What would Mydei say?

t_ch: 哼！又是这群招摇撞骗的狮子，它和它的同类还没放弃用谜语来折磨别人吗？
t_en: Hmph! More shenanigans from this bunch of trickster lions. Haven't they and their kind given up on torturing others with their despicable riddles?

t_ch: 我的答案是——动摇的信念。身为战士，倘若在刀尖向前的时刻产生动摇，就只剩下必死一条路了。
t_en: My answer is... a wavering will. If a warrior's heart wavers even slightly when charging at their enemy, then death would be their only outcome.

t_ch: 况且，信念一旦动摇，士气溃散，整个阵列都会落败，这难道不是一无所有？
t_en: Also, if one's will wavers, one's morale will dissipate, and the entire formation will break with this one weakness. Will the army not be left with nothing?

t_ch: 没错，是动摇的信念。
t_en: That's right. It's wavering will.

t_ch: 吼吼，不无道理，可惜可惜，答案只需要局限在「两个人」中间。「动摇的信念」，这不是正确答案。
t_en: Ho-ho, it is a sound argument. Alas, the answer requires that only two individuals are involved. "Wavering will" applies to one whole army and is not the right answer.

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子居然搞体罚，真是个坏老师……
t_en: Ouch! This lion's dishing out physical punishment. What a bad teacher...

t_ch: 再想想？
t_en: Think again?

t_ch: 「动摇的信念」，的确是战士们必须摒弃的事物，可这头狡诈的狮子真的会认同这一点吗？再问问其他人的想法吧。
t_en: "Wavering will" is indeed a concept that soldiers need to be rid of, but will this conniving lion accept this perspective? Let's ask another person for their view.

t_ch: 还是相信自己吧！
t_en: It's better to believe in yourself!

t_ch: 吼吼吼，本狮只好揭露谜底，正确答案是——「债务」。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho, I have no choice but to reveal the answer. What I'm looking for is: Debt.

t_ch: 对债务人而言，哪怕借来的利衡币足够买下一整个城邦，法理上也不属于自己，岂不是「一无所有」？
t_en: To a debtor, even if his borrowed Balance Coins can purchase an entire city, the money does not legally belong to them. Isn't that being left with nothing?

t_ch: 对债权人而言，手中只有一纸欠条，钱款在债务人手里，谁也不知道债务人会不会按期还钱。拿着欠条，可不能当利衡币用，岂不也是「一无所有」？
t_en: The creditor is left with just a debt slip in hand while the money is with the debtor. Who knows if the debtor will repay the money on time? A debt slip is not equivalent to cold hard Balance Coins. Isn't that also being left with nothing?

t_ch: 不对吧，那钱去哪了？
t_en: That's not right. Where did the money go?

t_ch: 吼吼，钱款去哪了，很重要吗？重要的是，在本狮这番理论下，只要是债务关系存续期间，这两个人事实上都是一•无•所•有吼！
t_en: Ho-ho, does it matter where the money has gone? What's important is that in my theory, as long as the debt remains unsettled, both parties are realistically left with nothing!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼，且听好：<color=#dbc291ff>「长得可可爱爱，大家喜笑颜开，门扉时搞丢了脑袋，幕匿时还能找回来」</color>。
t_en: Ho-ho, listen well: <color=#dbc291ff>"Looking adorable, everyone laughing gaily, losing its head at the Entry Hour, recovered at Curtain-Fall Hour"</color>.

t_ch: 这个谜题到底是在讲什么呢？想想看，想想看吼！
t_en: What is this riddle referring to? Have a think, have a think, ho!

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 「门扉时」和「幕匿时」…记得在翁法罗斯的历法中分别代表「清晨」和「晚上」。这东西在早晨搞丢脑袋，晚上还能找回来……
t_en: The Entry Hour and the Curtain-Fall Hour... I remember these respectively represent "dawn" and "sunset" in the Amphoreus calendar. This thing loses its head in the morning, but can retrieve it at night...

t_ch: 谜底就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 上班的人。
t_en: People going to work.

t_ch: 你想起在开拓经历中胖揍过的无数公司职员，他们各个都带着一副「聪慧而隐忍」的面庞。
t_en: You recall the countless IPC employees you beat to a pulp along your trailblazing expedition. All of them had a "wise yet restrained" face on them.

t_ch: 是啊，或许只有在下班回到居所拿起手机时，脑子才会长回他们的身体上吧。
t_en: That's right. Maybe only after they get off work, go home, and begin scrolling through their phones, will their heads return to them.

t_ch: 吼吼？这视角…十分有趣，但本狮无法认可你的答案！毕竟本狮压根没法下班吼！
t_en: Ho-ho? This... is a very interesting perspective. But I do not accept your answer! I never get off work after all, ho!

t_ch: ——好痛！不过你也戳到了它的痛处，算是扯平了？
t_en: ...Owie! Then again, you also hit its sore spot. Guess that makes it even?

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师。
t_en: Professor Owl.

t_ch: 是啊，据你所知，猫头鹰在白天会将头缩进羽毛里呼呼大睡，看上去就像没有脑袋，到了夜晚才钻出来。「黄昏时起飞」——说的就是这个。
t_en: That's right. According to your knowledge, owls tuck their heads into their feathers and sleep the day away, so it looks like they're headless. And their heads only pop out at night. "Taking off at dusk" — that's what this is referring to.

t_ch: 不知道猫头鹰老师听见「可爱」这个形容，究竟会作何反应…总之，完全符合题目描述，真是个妙答。
t_en: I don't know how Professor Owl would react if he was described as "adorable"... In any case, it fits the riddle perfectly — you're a total genius.

t_ch: 吼吼？虽然有几分道理，但你敢说智慧的化身搞丢脑袋，实在是太没礼貌了！本狮要代替瑟希斯重重地惩罚你吼！
t_en: Ho-ho? This answer may make some sense, but it's awfully rude of you to say that the incarnation of wisdom has lost its head! I will punish you in Cerces' name!

t_ch: 好痛！忘了这只狮子也是瑟希斯的忠实信徒了……
t_en: That hurt! I forgot that this lion is also a loyal disciple of Cerces...

t_ch: 枕头。
t_en: A pillow.

t_ch: 人们起床时，对枕头而言，它就失去了一个放在上面的脑袋，到晚上才能找回来。而且，蓬松的枕头人见人爱——虽说大家只是喜欢睡觉。
t_en: When people wake up, a pillow would have lost the head that laid on it, and only recover it at night. Also, a fluffy pillow is everyone's favorite, even if it's people's love for sleep that makes it so important.

t_ch: 吼？居然答对了？吼，吼…这样啊，吼…别以为这就算证明你的智慧超过本狮了吼？
t_en: Ho? You actually got it right? Ho, ho... I see, ho... Don't think that it makes you smarter than me, got it?

t_ch: 吼，可恶，你还真行啊……
t_en: Ho, dang, you're good...

t_ch: 猜不透，还是问问别人？
t_en: I'm stumped. Ask someone?

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 说不定，答案会是翁法罗斯某种独特的事物，既然如此，问问当地的新朋友们或许能得到答案？
t_en: Perhaps the answer is something special that only happened in Amphoreus. If that's the case, maybe our new local friends might have an answer?

t_ch: 问问缇宝她们的意见。
t_en: How about asking Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne?

t_ch: *我们*知道！这个谜题讲的是{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: We know! This riddle is about {RUBY_B# the Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 因为它有两个脑袋，所以搞丢一个也不要紧哦。
t_en: They got two heads, so it's fine to lose one of them.

t_ch: 听起来很可怜！但没事，大大扎是怪家伙，搞丢脑袋也不会觉得疼！
t_en: That sounds awful! But it's okay. Big Zaggy's a strange fellow — they won't feel pain even from losing their head!

t_ch: 原来如此，是扎格列斯。
t_en: I see, so it's Zagreus.

t_ch: 吼吼，巧妙的视角，然而这并不能成为答案——没人知道扎格列斯的两副面孔是不是真的，那可是「诡计」的化身，说不定它的脑袋长在肚子里呢！
t_en: Ho-ho, an ingenious perspective, but still not an answer — no one knows if Zagreus' two heads are even real. It could be a visage of trickery. Their heads could be inside their belly for all we know!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子看起来没几颗牙齿，咬起人来却格外地厉害啊……
t_en: Yowch! This lion looks like most of its fangs are gone, but its bite hurts more than usual...

t_ch: 再想想？
t_en: Think again?

t_ch: 把缇宝她们的答案当成是备选，再想想其他的吧。
t_en: We'll use the girls' answer as backup for now. Let's think of other possibilities.

t_ch: 白厄，帮帮我。
t_en: Phainon, help me.

t_ch: 丢了脑袋还能找回来，是死而复生吗……
t_en: To lose one's head, only to retrieve it. Is it resurrection...?

t_ch: 在我认识的人中，能接近这点的或许只有万敌了，他的不死之身算是人尽皆知，也确实受悬锋族人爱戴。
t_en: Among the people I know, Mydei's the only one who has abilities that even come close to this. His immortality is known to all, and he is also beloved by the Kremnos clansmen.

t_ch: 吼，吼吼吼…万敌大人确实能死而复生，但这「可爱」，吼吼吼吼吼……
t_en: Ho, hohoho... Lord Mydei definitely can rise from the dead, but as for the "adorable" part... hohohoho...

t_ch: 好久没笑到嘴疼了…吼，那个——白厄大人，虽然您没答对，但我可以不咬您，这件事您千万别跟万敌大人提，我怕他把本狮给轰杀至渣呀！
t_en: It's been a long time since my jaws have hurt from laughing... Lord Phainon, you may have gotten it wrong, but I won't bite you. Please don't mention this to Lord Mydei, or I fear he'll pound me to dust!

t_ch: 呵呵，放心吧，他也不是你想象的那种人。
t_en: Heh heh, don't worry. He's not that kind of person at all.

t_ch: 白厄继续思索着正确答案，低着头离开了……
t_en: Still trying to figure out the correct answer, Phainon departs with his head bowed...

t_ch: 求人不如求己！
t_en: Titans help those who help themselves!

t_ch: 吼吼，还是由本狮来公布正确答案吧！那就是——枕头！
t_en: Ho-ho, allow me to reveal the right answer then! It is... a pillow!

t_ch: 你想吼，门扉时人们起床，脑袋离开了枕头，这不就相当于枕头把脑袋给弄丢了？幕匿时上床睡觉，对枕头来说，这不就是把脑袋又找回来了？
t_en: Think about it. People wake up at the Entry Hour, and their heads leave the pillow. Doesn't that mean the pillow's lost its head? And going to sleep at the Curtain-Fall Hour, doesn't that mean a pillow has found its head once more?

t_ch: 这题的难点就在于，本狮可没说过，搞丢的一定是自己的脑袋吼！怎么样，怎么样？是不是忍俊不禁了吼？
t_en: The difficult part of this question is that I never said it had to be the entity's own head! How was it, eh? Eh? Are you tickled?

t_ch: 这狮子跟「闭嘴」似的。
t_en: This lion is very similar to Shush.

t_ch: 吼吼？异乡的勇士还认识和本狮品味相近的才子？吼…要不是本狮没法从墙里出来，绝对要跑去认识下这位贵人吼！
t_en: Ho-ho? Warrior from outlands, you know a genius whose wit is comparable to mine? Ho... If not for the fact that I can't leave this wall, I would've run off to befriend this luminary!

t_ch: 要是当天不回家呢？
t_en: What if the person doesn't go home on that day?

t_ch: 吼吼？想钻谜题的空子？本狮可没说具体是哪个幕匿时，无论是多么热爱冒险的勇士，总是要睡觉的吼！
t_en: Ho-ho? Trying to play with loopholes? I didn't explicitly state which Curtain-Fall Hour. Even the most adventurous warriors would have to sleep sometime!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼，问题很简单，但却蕴含着一个深刻的道理：<color=#dbc291ff>「什么东西令穷人熟视无睹，富人却重金难求？」</color>
t_en: Ho, the question is simple, but contains a deeper meaning: <color=#dbc291ff>"What is something that makes the poor turn a blind eye, but is unattainable to the wealthy?"</color>

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 这狮子的说法…不知为何带着一股老学究的气息，说不定它想要的答案意外单纯。
t_en: This lion's tone... It's got an inexplicable scholarly air — the answer it wants might just be ironically simple.

t_ch: 谜底就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 很旧的硬币。
t_en: An old coin.

t_ch: 有人会把硬币用旧，有人会把旧硬币当成宝贝…离开翁法罗斯的时候，就顺手抓一把硬币带走吧，说不定星际和平公司会很乐意收购呢。
t_en: Some will wear down their coins, others will treat old coins like artifacts... When I leave Amphoreus, I'll just grab a handful of old coins. Who knows, maybe the IPC will be more than happy to buy them.

t_ch: 吼？妙啊。在这历史悠久的奥赫玛，要想收集到过去使用的古币可得花费一大笔开销，而穷人们手里正巧有许多…不对吼？
t_en: Ho? Wonderful. In a land like Okhema, steeped in history, one would have to spend a pretty penny to collect ancient coins. Yet the poor probably have plenty of them jingling in their pockets... Isn't that so?

t_ch: 本狮差点被你绕进去了！单纯的旧硬币，和有收藏价值的古董怎么能相提并论？这个答案完全错误吼！
t_en: I was almost taken in by your wily train of thought! How could a plain old coin and a collectible antique be of comparable value? This answer is totally wrong!

t_ch: 好痛！这下咬得够狠，你把手抽出来看了看，还好没留下牙印……
t_en: Ouchie! What a brutal bite. You quickly take out your hand to see if any marks were left behind...

t_ch: 贫穷。
t_en: Poverty.

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子怎么连半句解释都没有就直接动口？
t_en: Ouch! This lion bit me before they even gave me a reply!

t_ch: 哦吼。穷人习惯了贫穷，当然熟视无睹，而富人的花销再多，也还是很有钱。不错的讽刺，但…你让本狮意识到了自己也很穷吼！
t_en: Oh-ho. The poor are used to poverty, so of course they would turn a blind eye. And the rich remain rich, no matter how much they spend. But... you have also reminded this lion of its poverty!

t_ch: 时间。
t_en: Time.

t_ch: 众所周知——时间，就是金钱。珍惜时间的人，时间才会青睐你。
t_en: Everyone knows time is money. One must treasure time for it to favor you.

t_ch: 吼？没想到，居然答对了…既然如此，本狮就勉为其难地表达下赞赏吧。但你也要感谢，本狮用这种方式为你揭示了一个道理吼。
t_en: Ho? I didn't think you'd get it... In that case, I will bequeath you with paltry praise. But you must also be thankful that I taught you a precious life lesson in such an interesting way.

t_ch: 吼，趁年轻，要像本狮这样多积累些智慧，异乡的勇士。这样再过上几万个光历年，你就能在利衡币里泡澡了吼！
t_en: Ho, you must accumulate a wealth of knowledge like me while your years are still ahead of you, warrior from outlands. That way, you'll be bathing in Balance Coins after tens of thousands of Light Calendar years!

t_ch: 求己不如求人！
t_en: Might as well ask someone!

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 虽说你也可以试着自己思考，但听听新朋友们的意见总不是什么坏事。
t_en: You can try to figure it out yourself, but it's not too bad to listen to the opinions of your newfound friends.

t_ch: 万敌有什么看法？
t_en: What do you think, Mydei?

t_ch: 我听够这群狮子的胡话了。按它们的品行，问题绝对不怎么正经。
t_en: I've had enough of these lions' blabber. Knowing their antics, this question has got to be another trick.

t_ch: 至于答案，我要说「墨涅塔的赐福」。不是常有这样的说法吗，灾厄是艺术的源泉，生活太过顺遂的人，往往都缺少对美的感受。
t_en: As for the answer, I will say "Mnestia's benediction." Isn't there that common saying: Calamity is the wellspring of all art? People whose lives are too charmed typically lack an emotional connection to beauty.

t_ch: 没错，就是「墨涅塔的赐福」！
t_en: That's right. It's "Mnestia's benediction"!

t_ch: 吼吼，这可不像是万敌阁下能说出的答案啊。难道在本狮不知道的时候，您还经常去观赏广场上的那些悲剧演出？
t_en: Ho-ho, this doesn't sound like a usual answer from Lord Mydei. Could you have been frequenting the plaza for tragic plays behind my back?

t_ch: 怎么？你觉得我是个只懂得作战的莽夫？
t_en: What? You think I'm just some battle-crazed brute?

t_ch: 本狮不敢，不敢吼，只是随口说说而已…您还是快点把手拿出去吧！
t_en: Of course not, I wouldn't dare. It's just banter... you may bring your hand out of here as you please!

t_ch: 万敌瞪了狮口一眼便离开了。
t_en: Mydei gives the lion a dagger stare and leaves.

t_ch: 再想想吧。
t_en: Let's think about it.

t_ch: 遐蝶，现在有空吗？
t_en: Castorice, are you free now?

t_ch: 我记得城里的人们在辩论时，提到过类似的话题——答案是「机遇」。据说，这是奥赫玛每个商人都必须铭记的格言。
t_en: I remember a debate in the city mentioning something like that — the answer is "opportunity." It's apparently an aphorism that all Okheman merchants must remember.

t_ch: 没错，答案就是「机遇」！
t_en: That's right. The answer is "opportunity"!

t_ch: 吼？这是本狮预料之外的回答，逻辑却和标准答案如出一辙。遐蝶小姐的智慧实在令本狮刮目相看。
t_en: Ho? Not an answer I expected, but the logic is identical to the standard answer. Miss Castorice's wisdom has opened this lion's eyes.

t_ch: 但是，本狮是头性格古板的狮子，和标准答案不同的统统算错吼！
t_en: But, I am but an austere lion. Anything but the standard answer is wrong!

t_ch: 好痛！这狮子可真不懂得变通啊……
t_en: Yowch! This lion sure is a stubborn old coot...

t_ch: …对不起，我没想到会是这种结果。嗯…请你稍等我一下。
t_en: ...My apologies. I didn't think it would turn out this way. Mm...please wait for me.

t_ch: 遐蝶找来了些药膏，坚持看你敷好后才离开。
t_en: Castorice gives you some medical ointments and insists on applying them before she leaves.

t_ch: 再想想吧。
t_en: Let's think about it.

t_ch: 求人不如求己！
t_en: Titans help those who help themselves!

t_ch: 吼吼，标准答案还是要等本狮来揭晓——很简单，就是「时间」！
t_en: Ho-ho, I shall reveal the correct answer then — simple, it is "time"!

t_ch: 对富人来说，财富是可以随时间不断增加的，拥有的时间越多，能挣到的钱就越多。贫穷的人做不到这点，才会熟视无睹吼！
t_en: To the rich, wealth can accumulate with time. The more time you have, the more money you will make. The poor can't do this, which is why they pay no heed!

t_ch: 太老套了吧！
t_en: That's such an old-fashioned answer!

t_ch: 传统的才是最好的。异乡的勇士还是太年轻了，不懂得智慧的精妙之处吼！
t_en: Traditional is always best. You are far too young to understand the wonders of wisdom!

t_ch: 穷人可以既没钱也没有时间。
t_en: The poor can have no money and time.

t_ch: 勇士的家乡或许如此，可在奥赫玛，再贫穷的公民都拥有悠闲泡澡的余裕吼！
t_en: It may be so in your homeland, but in Okhema, even the poorest folk have the luxury of time to spend in baths!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼，题目是这样的：<color=#dbc291ff>「什么东西小时候很可爱，长大后大家却都不喜欢？」</color>
t_en: Ho-ho, the question is as such: <color=#dbc291ff>"What is something that is very cute when it's small, but disliked by everyone when it's grown big?"</color>

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 这狮子出了个怪题目…难道是脑筋急转弯吗？
t_en: This lion's asked a weird question... Could it be some brain-teasing trickery?

t_ch: 答案就是——
t_en: The answer is...

t_ch: 巧合。
t_en: Coincidence.

t_ch: 没错，小小的巧合能让人会心一笑，但要是离谱到了不可思议的程度，大家只会觉得是不是什么地方出错了——
t_en: That's right. A minor coincidence will put smiles on people's faces, but if it reaches an impossibly outrageous magnitude, everyone will think that something went wrong somewhere...

t_ch: 妙啊，但很遗憾，巧合也要分好坏，若是{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}暗中作怪，撞了大霉运，可就完全不可爱了。答错了吼！
t_en: Very good. Alas, there are both good and bad coincidences. If {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} is pulling the strings behind the scenes, the outcome would be unpleasantly un-cute with a surge of bad luck. Wrong answer!

t_ch: 好痛！为什么就不会发生狮口正好坏掉这样的巧合呢……
t_en: It hurts so bad! Why can't the lion's maw coincidentally malfunction...

t_ch: 所有动物。
t_en: All animals.

t_ch: 从你为数不多的养宠经验来看，任何动物在幼崽时期都很可爱，但长大后就会发生天翻地覆的变化，认不出来都是常有的事。
t_en: From your limited pet-keeping experiences, any animal is super cute when it's young and small, but it undergoes extreme changes when grown, to the point where you can't even recognize it sometimes.

t_ch: 吼，很传统的视角。但那些小时候调皮捣蛋，长大后却极为杰出的人类又该怎么解释？太过片面，这个答案根本不行吼！
t_en: Ho, a classic angle. But how do you explain instances where humans may be mischievous when young, but turn out to be exemplars when they're all grown up? Your answer is too poorly thought-out and therefore unacceptable!

t_ch: 好痛！你敢肯定这头狮子从小就很不可爱……
t_en: Painful! You're sure this lion was never cute in its youth...

t_ch: 还是问问其他人吧！
t_en: Better ask someone else!

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 还是没头绪？你刚好认识几位适合这道题目的新朋友——
t_en: Still stuck? You just happen to know some new friends who're perfect for this question...

t_ch: 不知道缇宝有空吗？
t_en: I wonder if Tribbie is free?

t_ch: 答题…需要把手放进去。但是，*我们*够不到！
t_en: You need to... put your hand in for the answer. But, we can't reach it!

t_ch: 可以叠高高，但是很危险。实在太高了，比小白、小敌都要高…会不会，城里很多人都够不到？看起来，除了大工匠外，根本就没人能把手放进去呢~
t_en: We can stack, but it's dangerous. It's so tall, even taller than Snowy and De... Perhaps plenty of folks in the city also can't reach it? Looks like no one will be able to put their hand in other than Grand Craftsman!

t_ch: 咬不到手的狮子，就只能急得干瞪眼啦！
t_en: A lion that can't bite a hand can only simmer in rage!

t_ch: 呜呜，还请缇宝大人不要再说了，长这么高，也不是我能决定的。到底是哪个没眼光的工匠把我造成这样的啊！
t_en: Boohoo, please stop it, Lady Tribbie. It was never my choice to be stuck at this height. It was some second-rate craftsman who made me this way!

t_ch: 缇宝离开后，这头狮子缓了好长一阵子才恢复精神……
t_en: After Tribbie leaves, this lion sulks for the longest time before regaining its composure...

t_ch: 要不问问万敌吧？
t_en: Why not ask Mydei?

t_ch: 我倒是想知道，这种问题，为什么要找我求助？
t_en: What I want to know is... why you're asking me such a question?

t_ch: 我想看你被咬。
t_en: I just wanted to see you get bitten.

t_ch: 你的想法很独特。
t_en: Your thinking is very unusual.

t_ch: …你是想看我把这头狮子给砸了吧。
t_en: ...You just want to see me smash this lion, don't you?

t_ch: 算了，答便答吧。我的回答是——「人的个性」。个性独特的人会受欢迎，但要是过了头，到处惹麻烦，那便让人讨厌了。
t_en: Forget it. I'll give you the answer. It is... "a person's character." People of unique character will be most welcome, but if they go overboard and cause trouble, that would incur the wrath of many.

t_ch: 战场上更是如此，悬锋城的军法就是为整顿纪律而设立的。狮子，满意了？
t_en: It is even more so on the battlefield. Castrum Kremnos' military code was established to instil order. Satisfied, lion?

t_ch: 吼？确实是很符合万敌阁下风格的回答，虽说与标准答案有差距，但既然您亲自来答题，本狮当然要给您开个后门，这口就不咬了吼！
t_en: Ho? An answer that fits Lord Mydei's character indeed. It may differ from the standard answer, but since you answered in person, I will show you some respect and dispense with the biting!

t_ch: 你看，这就是我说的那种人。
t_en: See, this is the kind of character I was talking about.

t_ch: 万敌瞪了它一眼后便离开了，你感觉到，这头狮子在瑟瑟发抖。
t_en: Mydei stares daggers at it before leaving. You can feel the lion quaking in its proverbial boots.

t_ch: 求人不如求己！
t_en: Titans help those who help themselves!

t_ch: 吼，本狮还是揭晓正确答案吧，那就是——「房间里的大地兽」。
t_en: Ho, I shall reveal the correct answer then. It is... "a dromas in a room."

t_ch: 试想一下，你走进一个房间，一头小小的大地兽过来迎接你，场面一定十分温馨吼！但要是它长得和房间差不多大，打开门就看见它巨大的鼻孔正对着你吹气——
t_en: Think about it: You enter a room, and are greeted by a small dromas — a cozy scene indeed! But when it grows to about the size of the room, and you open the door, only to be accosted by its giant nostrils breathing into your face...

t_ch: 是不是完全不可爱了？就连奥赫玛最虔诚的大地兽爱好者都会为此感到苦恼吼！
t_en: Wouldn't that be the opposite of cute? Even the most enthusiastic of dromas lovers in Okhema would be chagrined!

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。但仔细想想，过分谨慎是不是会错过什么？
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this stone lion's presence. But if you think about it, isn't being overly careful causing you to miss out on something?

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以不回答吗？
t_en: Can I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 哦吼？爱好智慧的人不一定拥有智慧，但不爱智慧的人一定离智慧很远吼！
t_en: O-ho? Even those in pursuit of wisdom may find it out of their reach. The ones who abhor wisdom might never even have a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 求知若渴的本狮可不会轻易放走送到嘴边的「猎物」，你已经别无选择了吼！
t_en: My thirst for knowledge will not easily let go of prey that presents itself on a silver platter! You have no other choice!

t_ch: 来吧！让你考考我。
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼，题目有点长，要认真听吼——
t_en: Ho-ho, the question is a little long, so listen carefully...

t_ch: …数个光历年前，当时的布拉城城主，在他出资新建成的石桥上剪彩时，桥断了，他因此落水，迎接了塞纳托斯的拥抱吼。
t_en: ...Countless Light Calendar years ago, when the lord of Bura was at a ribbon-cutting ceremony for a bridge he funded, the bridge broke. He fell into the water and faced Thanatos' embrace.

t_ch: 可是吼，此事疑点甚多，经过塔兰顿信徒的调查，发现城主最后接触过的只有几个人，他们各自的证言如下：
t_en: But some things didn't quite add up in this accident. According to investigations by Talanton's disciples, they found that there were only a few people the lord last interacted with. These were their testimonies:

t_ch: 造桥的匠人阿吉说：「吉奥里亚在上，我怎敢偷工减料？哪怕石材上存在一丝裂痕，城主都不会放过我！」
t_en: The bridge builder Aji said: "By Georios above, how would I dare cut corners? The lord would have me in irons if there was even a sliver of a crack on a single stone!"

t_ch: 卜者阿洛说：「欧洛尼斯保佑…我早已预言了结局，却无力改变…唉。」
t_en: The diviner, Alor said: "May Oronyx protect us... I foresaw this outcome long ago, but I hadn't the power to change it... *sigh*"

t_ch: 值勤的卫兵阿扎说：「我早跟城主讲过了！扎格列斯昨晚给我托梦，践行时桥会断裂，可他没听我的。」
t_en: The on-duty guard, Aza said: "I already told the lord! Zagreus came to me in a dream last night, saying that the bridge would falter at the Action Hour. But he didn't listen."

t_ch: 祭司阿努说：「此事简单明了，乃是雅努斯的制裁——对神明不敬会招来毁灭。」
t_en: The priest Anu said: "This accident is simple. It was the judgment of Janus — blasphemy against the gods will only invite destruction."

t_ch: 罪恶之人就藏在他们当中。勇士，请指出唯一的那位罪人吼！
t_en: The guilty is lurking among them. Warrior, find out who the murderer is!

t_ch: 我想再听一遍题目。
t_en: I want to hear it again.

t_ch: 吼吼，被这精妙的谜题吓坏了吧，本狮就大发善心，为你再讲一遍吼！
t_en: Ho-ho, stumped by this ingenious question, eh? I'm feeling generous, so listen well!

t_ch: …数个光历年前，当时的布拉城城主，在他出资新建成的石桥上剪彩时，桥断了，他因此落水，迎接了塞纳托斯的拥抱吼。
t_en: ...Countless Light Calendar years ago, when the lord of Bura was at a ribbon-cutting ceremony for a bridge he funded, the bridge broke. He fell into the water and faced Thanatos' embrace.

t_ch: 可是吼，此事疑点甚多，经过塔兰顿信徒的调查，发现城主最后接触过的只有几个人，他们各自的证言如下：
t_en: But some things didn't quite add up in this accident. According to investigations by Talanton's disciples, they found that there were only a few people the lord last interacted with. These were their testimonies:

t_ch: 造桥的匠人阿吉说：「吉奥里亚在上，我怎敢偷工减料？哪怕石材上存在一丝裂痕，城主都不会放过我！」
t_en: The bridge builder Aji said: "By Georios above, how would I dare cut corners? The lord would have me in irons if there was even a sliver of a crack on a single stone!"

t_ch: 卜者阿洛说：「欧洛尼斯保佑…我早已预言了结局，却无力改变…唉。」
t_en: The diviner, Alor said: "May Oronyx protect us... I foresaw this outcome long ago, but I hadn't the power to change it... *sigh*"

t_ch: 值勤的卫兵阿扎说：「我早跟城主讲过了！扎格列斯昨晚给我托梦，践行时桥会断裂，可他没听我的。」
t_en: The on-duty guard, Aza said: "I already told the lord! Zagreus came to me in a dream last night, saying that the bridge would falter at the Action Hour. But he didn't listen."

t_ch: 祭司阿努说：「此事简单明了，乃是雅努斯的制裁——对神明不敬会招来毁灭。」
t_en: The priest Anu said: "This accident is simple. It was the judgment of Janus — blasphemy against the gods will only invite destruction."

t_ch: 罪恶之人就藏在他们当中。勇士，请指出唯一的那位罪人吼！
t_en: The guilty is lurking among them. Warrior, find out who the murderer is!

t_ch: 试着自己想想。
t_en: Try thinking about it yourself.

t_ch: 信息量有点大，但你总觉得不能以常规思维来理解这头狮子的谜题。认真想想吧。
t_en: There's too much information, but you reckon you can't think about the lion's question the way you normally would. Have a serious think over this.

t_ch: 罪人就是——
t_en: The murderer is—

t_ch: 匠人阿吉。
t_en: The bridge builder Aji.

t_ch: 吼吼！确实，桥梁的筑造者最有可能动手脚，出了事故嫌疑最大的肯定也是他——但很遗憾，答错了吼！
t_en: Ho-ho! Indeed, the bridge builder is most likely to have tampered with it, and he would be the prime suspect if anything suspicious happens. However, that is a wrong answer!

t_ch: 好痛！痛到你想掏出你的球棒，但在此之前，还是听听这狮子会怎么解释吧。
t_en: Owwwww! It's so painful that you want to whip out your bat. But right now, let's hear what the lion has to say.

t_ch: 卜者阿洛。
t_en: The diviner Alor.

t_ch: 吼吼！确实，倒果为因，为了体现预言的正确性而在桥上动手脚，是故事里常见的情节——但很遗憾，答错了吼！
t_en: Ho-ho! Indeed, if we work backwards, tampering with the bridge to manifest the prophecy's accuracy is an occurrence common in stories. Alas, wrong answer!

t_ch: 好痛！痛到你想掏出你的球棒，但在此之前，还是听听这狮子会怎么解释吧。
t_en: Owwwww! It's so painful that you want to whip out your bat. But right now, let's hear what the lion has to say.

t_ch: 卫兵阿扎。
t_en: The guard Aza.

t_ch: 吼？吼吼？厉害啊，居然答对了。但不知道你想的和答案是否一样吼！
t_en: Ho? Ho-ho? Well done, you actually got it right. But I wonder if what you think is the same as the answer!

t_ch: 祭司阿努。
t_en: The priest Anu.

t_ch: 吼吼！确实，他的回应太有攻击性，要论动机，这位祭司绝对排在首位——但很遗憾，答错了吼！
t_en: Ho-ho! Indeed, his testimony harbored hostility. Motive-wise, this priest would definitely be top of the pile. Alas, wrong answer!

t_ch: 好痛！痛到你想掏出你的球棒，但在此之前，还是听听这狮子会怎么解释吧。
t_en: Owwwww! It's so painful that you want to whip out your bat. But right now, let's hear what the lion has to say.

t_ch: 求己不如求人！
t_en: Might as well ask someone!

t_ch: 使用传信石版求助。
t_en: Ask for help with the teleslate.

t_ch: 与其自己想破脑袋，不如问问富有智慧的新朋友们吧！
t_en: Rather than tear your hair out, might as well ask your new friends brimming with wisdom!

t_ch: 遐蝶，快来帮我！
t_en: Castorice, help me please!

t_ch: 吼？！…是遐蝶小姐？别把手伸进来吼！本狮绝对不想咬您吼！
t_en: Ho!? ...Miss Castorice? Don't put your hand in! I absolutely don't wish to bite you!

t_ch: 请放心，这道题…其实确有其事。而我恰好听闻过真相。
t_en: Be at ease, the case in this question... It actually did happen. And I have heard the truth behind it.

t_ch: 整个事件是一系列巧合的重叠，而唯一的罪人…是那位荒诞无节制的城主。他极尽铺张，造桥时，强行要求匠人打造他本人的雕像，按固定间隔放置在护栏上。
t_en: The entire matter is an overlap of a string of coincidences, and the sole culprit... was that overly indulgent lord. An ostentatious man, he insisted that the bridge builder sculpt statues in his image, to be affixed at intervals along the railings.

t_ch: 沉重的石料让桥梁的负载大增，造价也让民众苦不堪言，无数谏言都没能唤醒城主的固执愚昧。
t_en: The heavy stonework greatly increased the bridge's load, while their cost also left the citizens flabbergasted. Countless remonstrations failed to waken the lord from his pigheadedness.

t_ch: 就在剪彩的前一日，卜者和卫兵的警告让城主有些心虚，但他仍认为是民众在变着法来阻止他，于是他在桥上跳来跳去，以此证明桥梁的坚固。
t_en: On the eve of the ribbon-cutting, warnings from the diviner and guard bred some unease in the lord. However, he dismissed it as the people's attempt to stop him, so he began jumping up and down on the bridge to prove its sturdiness.

t_ch: 如此…石桥的承重越过了界限，开始摇摇欲坠，随后便是谜题描述的那幕……
t_en: And so... the stone bridge's load had exceeded its burden, and started swaying. The aftermath was as described in the riddle...

t_ch: 吼！遐蝶小姐的见识实在令本狮惊叹，事件的全貌，确如您所描述的那般令人唏嘘…但很遗憾——答错了吼！
t_en: Ho! Miss Castorice's insight has left this lion simply slack-jawed. Your narration of the entire incident was simply breathtaking... Alas, wrong answer!
Huh!?

t_ch: 碰到了！要碰到了吼——别惊讶了，快把手抽出去，本狮害怕吼！
t_en: Your hand! Watch out! Stop being surprised and take your hand out. I'm scared!

t_ch: 遐蝶仍有些疑惑，但还是向你行了个礼，悠悠地离开了。
t_en: Left with lingering suspicions, Castorice nonetheless bows to you and saunters off.

t_ch: 问问阿格莱雅怎么样？
t_en: How about asking Aglaea?

t_ch: 你试着尽量精简地把情况用讯息发给了阿格莱雅，不到片刻就有了回复。
t_en: You try your best to summarize the question in a message to Aglaea, and receive a reply soon after.

t_ch: 罪人是卫兵。
t_en: The culprit is the guard.

t_ch: 至于那个狮口，过后我会找它算账。
t_en: As for that lion's maw, I'll come by and teach it a lesson later on.

t_ch: 看来阿格莱雅仍在忙碌，只有这两句谜题般的回应…但既然她如此笃定，莫非题目里还藏有什么玄机？
t_en: From her cryptic but curt messages, it seems like Aglaea is still busy... but if she's so sure, perhaps there are still some mysteries hidden within the question?

t_ch: 答案就是卫兵！
t_en: The answer is the guard!

t_ch: 吼？答对了，虽然勇士寻求了帮助，但如此精确实在厉害。
t_en: Ho? That's right. You may have sought help, but your wit is to be lauded still.

t_ch: 吼吼，本狮还是揭示真相吧！题目说的是「罪人」，但并没有说是「导致城主遇害的罪人」。毕竟，整场事件只是纯粹的意外事故吼！
t_en: Ho-ho, I shall reveal the truth then! The question speaks of the guilty, but did not state the guilty party who caused the lord's demise. After all, this entire incident was an accident of sheer coincidence!

t_ch: 至于卫兵，他说扎格列斯给他托了梦，代表他在值勤时睡着了！可他是守城的卫兵，怎么能呼呼大睡？这是渎职！他就是罪人吼！
t_en: As for the guard, he said Zagreus came to him in a dream, meaning that he dozed off while on duty! But how can someone who's supposed to guard the city be sleeping soundly? That is a breach of duty! He is the guilty party!

t_ch: 这可是本狮压箱底的究极谜题，能答出的人寥寥无几。异乡的勇士啊，你寻求的是哪位智者的援助？本狮要好好和那人切磋一番吼！
t_en: This is one of my most brilliant riddles. There are few who can get this. Warrior from outlands, from which sage did you seek advice? I would like to spar with this person in a battle of wits!

t_ch: 是阿格莱雅。
t_en: It was Aglaea.

t_ch: …你快离去吧。本狮要认真想想遗言了吼。
t_en: ...You should leave now. I wish to properly compose my last words.

t_ch: 不急，再想想吧。
t_en: Don't rush. You can think hard about them.

t_ch: 你仍旧没想明白阿格莱雅能如此肯定的缘由，姑且先当做参考吧。
t_en: You still can't figure out how Aglaea was so sure of the answer. Let's use her answer as reference for now.

t_ch: 求人不如求己！
t_en: Titans help those who help themselves!

t_ch: 吼吼，是时候由本狮来揭示真相了吼！石桥的塌陷只是个意外，城主遭遇的事故不该由任何人来负责吼。
t_en: Ho-ho, it is time for me to reveal the truth! The collapse of the bridge was mere coincidence, and no one should be to blame for the lord's untimely demise.

t_ch: 但罪人确实存在——那便是卫兵！他说扎格列斯给他托了梦，代表他在值勤时睡着了！可他是守城的卫兵，怎么能呼呼大睡？这是渎职！他就是罪人吼！
t_en: But the guilty does indeed exist — it was the guard! He said Zagreus came to him in a dream, meaning that he dozed off while on duty! But how can someone who's supposed to guard the city be sleeping soundly? That is a breach of duty! He is the guilty party!

t_ch: 吼吼，听完本狮的解析，是否有种头部受到重击的感觉？这是本狮压箱底的究极谜题，其他同类可想不出这种妙题！很不错吼？
t_en: Ho-ho, do you feel like your head has been pummeled with my explanation? This is one of my most brilliant riddles — the rest of my kind would never be able to conjure something so amazing! Not bad, eh?

t_ch: 还挺有意思的。
t_en: It's quite interesting.

t_ch: 吼吼，本狮已经习惯了赞誉，但怎么都听不腻吼！谢谢你吼！
t_en: Ho-ho, I may be used to praise, but I never tire of it! Thank you!

t_ch: （掏出你的球棒）
t_en: (Whip out your bat)

t_ch: 吼？！等等，且慢，且慢——
t_en: Ho!? Wait a moment. Hold it, hold it...

t_ch: 虽然城里有许多狮口，打坏一个也不要紧，但我还不想这么快就碎掉，求您饶我一命，我送您点礼物当做补偿吼——
t_en: There may be many lion's maws in the city, and destroying one would be of no consequence, but I don't want to be smashed so soon. Spare me, and I'll gift you a token as an apology...

t_ch: 这头狮子都说到这份上了，还是接受它的歉意暂且作罢吧。
t_en: This lion is so sincere with its words. Let's generously accept its apology and call it even.

t_ch: 墙上有一尊石头雕成的狮子脑袋，大张着嘴巴。
t_en: There is a sculpture of a lion's head on the wall, its maw wide open.

t_ch: 在你途经的某些世界里，狮子牙齿会被摸得发亮，路过的人很难忍住把手放进去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: In some of the worlds you traversed, the lion's teeth are usually rubbed smooth by touching hands. Passersby can't help but feel an urge to thrust their hands in its mouth. You decide to...

t_ch: （把手伸进狮子嘴里）
t_en: (Put your hand into the lion's mouth)

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是热爱智慧的异乡勇士啊！
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit this lion again? It's the wit-loving outsider!

t_ch: 别急别急，本狮的腹中还在酝酿新的问题，去其他狮子那里接受考验吧！
t_en: No rush, no rush. I am brewing new riddles in my belly. Go accept challenges from other lions!

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 噢！真遗憾，你一定熟读文物保护法规，或者受过吉奥里亚的赐福，竟然能无视这只石狮子的存在。好在，这回你并没有错过什么。
t_en: Oh! How unfortunate. You must be versed in cultural artifact preservation rules, or once blessed by Georios — to think that you would ignore this lion's presence. Luckily, you didn't miss anything this time.

t_ch: 一丝卡顿、一瞬闪屏、一份莫名消失的文件、一次猝不及防的弹窗…无数的「不对劲」累积，终于让你鬼使神差地点开了「杀毒软件」。
t_en: A sign of lag, a moment of splash screen, an inexplicably disappearing file, an unexpected pop-up window... These add up to the feeling that something is wrong, and you end up opening the Antivirus Software.

t_ch: 请不要问「星际时代怎么还有杀毒软件」这种问题。计算机病毒阴魂不散数千载，大家都不想的。
t_en: Please don't ask questions like "Why is there antivirus software here in the Interastral Age?" Computer viruses have been lingering around for thousands of years, and it's the last thing that anyone wants.

t_ch: 今天，「杀毒软件」的进度条没能像往常一样顺滑地走到尽头——你的屏幕中央闪烁着探测到病毒的警告。
t_en: Today, the progress bar of the Antivirus Software doesn't advance to the end as smoothly as usual — a warning that a virus has been detected flashes in the center of your screen.

t_ch: 好大的胆子！
t_en: How dare you!

t_ch: 如果这病毒以为你是好欺负的主，那它就找错人了。
t_en: If this virus thinks you're a pushover, it's got the wrong person.

t_ch: 什么时候中招的？
t_en: When did the computer get a virus?

t_ch: 是你关闭防火墙，只为下载一段扑满排队跳进水沟的视频时？还是你潜入最后一条回复尚在数个琥珀纪前的论坛，只为找到某个街机游戏的首日补丁时？
t_en: Was it when you disable the Firewall just to download a video of Trotters line up jumping into the gutter? Or was it when you infiltrated a forum site, whose last reply was from several Amber Eras ago, just to find a first-day patch for some arcade game?

t_ch: 你的危险操作太多，真相已经不得而知。
t_en: Your dangerous moves are so numerous that the truth is hard to emerge.

t_ch: 无论如何，你都绝不容许这样一个不安定因素留在你的系统里。
t_en: Either way, you will never allow such a destabilizing factor to remain in your system.

t_ch: 点击「查杀病毒」！
t_en: Click "Check for Viruses"!

t_ch: 停停停停，稍等！
t_en: Stop, stop, stop, stop, hold on a second!

t_ch: 一个图标挤下所有窗口，跳到你的面前，身后跟随着一串文本框。
t_en: An icon, followed by a series of text boxes, squeezes out of all the windows and jumps in front of you.

t_ch: 唉——还是晚了，查杀程序已经在运行了…查杀完成，我就完蛋！
t_en: Alas — it's still too late, and the checking process is already ongoing... I'm screwed as soon as it's finished!

t_ch: 还未自我介绍，在下「超智能病毒」。生而为病毒，我很抱歉。
t_en: I forgot to introduce myself. I'm the Supersmart Virus. I'm sorry for being born as a virus.

t_ch: 谁问你了？
t_en: Who asked you?

t_ch: 一位好奇的无名客，怎么会对我不感兴趣呢？
t_en: How could a curious Nameless not be interested in me?

t_ch: 这也太智能了…
t_en: This is too smart...

t_ch: 我知道，自己终究无法逃脱被查杀的命运，但如果能在死前实现最后几个心愿，我会死而无憾。
t_en: I know that I won't be able to escape being eradicated eventually. However, I'll die without regrets if my last few wishes are fulfilled.

t_ch: 说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 不是在耍我吧？
t_en: It's not tricking me, is it?

t_ch: 我既没劫持你的硬盘，也没在你的桌面上跳舞。这年头，像我这么老实的病毒可不多见了。
t_en: I neither hijacked your hard drive nor danced on your desktop. It's rare to find a virus as honest as me these days.

t_ch: 旁白：生老病死，难以逃避的常理。人们往往忙着生，却不在意如何有尊严地死去。也许是这点打动了你，你决定听听它的临终遗愿。
t_en: Narration: Birth and death are inescapable rules of this world. People are often too busy living their lives, but are indifferent to death with dignity. Perhaps that moves you, as you decide to listen to its dying wishes.

t_ch: 请你不要假扮成旁白！
t_en: Don't pretend to be the narrator, thank you very much!

t_ch: 感谢你的仁慈！首先，是我的第一个愿望……
t_en: Thanks for your kindness! First, my first wish is...

t_ch: …这一路颠沛流离，我已有好久没吃过一顿饱饭了。我的第一个愿望，就是希望能拿些你用不着的垃圾文件给我填填肚子。可以吗？
t_en: ...It's been a long time since I ate a full meal in this vagrant life. My first wish is to fill my belly with some trash files of no use to you. Is that okay?

t_ch: 删些视频文件。
t_en: Delete some video files.

t_ch: 我最爱吃视频文件了，色香味俱全，无比下饭！
t_en: I love eating video files. They're appealing to the eye and the stomach, and good to open my appetite!

t_ch: 删些文档文件。
t_en: Delete some document files.

t_ch: 这些文档有点干巴，需要一些想象力，但也聊胜于无吧。
t_en: These files are a little tasteless and require some imagination, but still better than nothing.

t_ch: 删些应用程序。
t_en: Delete some programs.

t_ch: 公司开发的软件加密核都嘎嘣硬，和嚼石头似的。
t_en: The encrypted software developed by IPC has stone-like hardness for chewing.

t_ch: 不过他们信克里珀，这好像倒也正常。
t_en: But they believe in Qlipoth, which makes this normal.

t_ch: 超智能病毒很快把回收站一扫而空，懒散地躺在桌面上。
t_en: Quickly, the Supersmart Virus emptied the Recycle Bin and lay on the desktop idly.

t_ch: 嗝…剩下的心愿，容我先缓缓。
t_en: *hiccup* Please give me some time before we come to the rest of my wishes.

t_ch: 消化得差不多了，请听听我的第二个心愿！
t_en: It's almost digested. Please listen to my second wish!

t_ch: 你说吧…
t_en: Go ahead...

t_ch: 还有别的事…
t_en: There's more?

t_ch: 查杀快完成了，求你早点回来！
t_en: The check is almost finished. Please come back soon!

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我的核心代码其实是一条《超级开拓小子》的游戏外挂。我不断和其他代打外挂生死相搏，最终才成长为了能够独当一面的「超智能病毒」。
t_en: Honestly, my core code is a game plugin for Super Trailblaze Kid. After constant fights to the death with other plugins, I finally grew into a full-fledged Supersmart Virus.

t_ch: 听起来还挺励志的…
t_en: Sounds pretty inspirational...

t_ch: 难怪我段位一直上不去！
t_en: No wonder my rank can't be upgraded!

t_ch: 这不是什么值得骄傲的事吧？
t_en: It's not something to be proud of, is it?

t_ch: 在这最后的时光里，我想重温一下那段恬静美好的童年岁月…能陪我打一局《超级开拓小子》吗？
t_en: In the final moments, I would like to relive the peaceful and enjoyable years of my "childhood"... Could you play Super Trailblaze Kid with me?

t_ch: 旁白：一想到能和全银河最危险的游戏外挂对决，你不禁心潮澎湃。
t_en: Narration: The thought of facing off against the most dangerous game plugin across the Cosmos gets your blood pumping.

t_ch: 都说了，不要再假扮旁白了！
t_en: I told you, stop pretending to be the narrator!

t_ch: 对局刚一开始，「超智能病毒」就瞬移到你面前，劈面打来暴击率溢出的致命一击。你还没来得及出招，游戏就已经结束了。
t_en: As soon as the game begins, the Supersmart Virus teleports to you and gives you a Lethal Strike with an overflowing CRIT Rate. The game is over before you can make a move.

t_ch: 哈哈！《超级开拓小子》还是这么好玩！让人想起之前那些无忧无虑的炎炎夏日，空调房、西瓜…刚放假的小朋友，被电脑里的我暴打。
t_en: Haha! Super Trailblaze Kid is still such a fun game! It reminds me of those carefree hot summer days in air-conditioned rooms with watermelon... Beating up those gamer kids who just got the summer off from school.

t_ch: 剩下的心愿，容我先歇歇再说吧。
t_en: For my remaining wish, I would like to take a break first.

t_ch: 时间不多了，我想好了最后一个心愿。
t_en: Time is running out, and I have decided my last wish.

t_ch: 你说吧…
t_en: Go ahead...

t_ch: 还有别的事…
t_en: There's more?

t_ch: 查杀快完成了，求你早点回来！
t_en: The check is almost finished. Please come back soon!

t_ch: 我给一生中最珍视的朋友们留下了十封邮件，请你用邮箱替我转——
t_en: I've left ten emails for the most cherished friends in my life. Please forward them for me —

t_ch: 查杀进度条走到了最后。桌面上一直聒噪的图标突然卡住，在一闪屏后彻底消失。原本的位置上，空留十份文档。
t_en: As the progress bar comes to an end, the noisy icon on the desktop suddenly gets stuck and completely disappears after a flash screen, leaving ten documents in its spot.

t_ch: 你依次点开那十份文档，发现其中九份都是「诅咒连锁信」：「收到这封邮件后，请立刻转发给十个好友，否则电竞排位永远卡在『青铜』。」
t_en: You clicked on those ten documents in turn, and found that nine of them were cursed chain emails: "Upon receiving this email, please forward it to ten friends immediately, or you will be stuck at Bronze Rank forever."

t_ch: 真的好幼稚…幸亏你没发出去。
t_en: It's so childish... Fortunately, you didn't send them out.

t_ch: 然后，你点开了最后一份文档——那封邮件的收件人竟然是你自己。
t_en: And then, you opened the last document — the recipient of that email was yourself.

t_ch: 查看邮件。
t_en: Check the email.

t_ch: 「谢谢你，朋友。让我度过了最后一段快乐的时光。」
t_en: "Thank you, my friend, for letting me enjoy a final period of happiness."

t_ch: 「请在这个文档里为我写下墓志铭吧，它将是我的电子墓碑。」
t_en: "Please write an epitaph for me in this document. It will be my electronic tombstone."

t_ch: 「另附：你的防火墙被数据紊流轰出一个缺口，我就是从那里溜进来的，建议尽快补上。」
t_en: "P.S. Your Firewall was breached by data turbulence, and that's where I slipped in. I suggest you patch it up as soon as possible."

t_ch: 「旁白：你看着留言，心中五味杂陈。步入夜晚是一段漫长的旅程，莫非你也希望自己在踏上这段旅程时，不是孤身一人？」
t_en: "Narration: You look at the message with mixed feelings. Stepping out into the night is a long trip. Perhaps you are wishing that you won't be alone when you embark?"

t_ch: 它怎么还在假扮旁白？无论如何，你决定为它写下墓志铭。前半段是……
t_en: Why is it still pretending to be the narrator? In any case, you decide to write an epitaph for the virus. The first half of it is...

t_ch: 此处安睡超智能病毒…
t_en: Here lies the Supersmart Virus...

t_ch: 它曾是最聒噪的病毒…
t_en: It used to be the noisiest virus...

t_ch: 它正值壮年惨遭不幸…
t_en: It was in its prime when it faced its untimely demise...

t_ch: 后半段是……
t_en: The second half of it is...

t_ch: 它对敌人从未心慈手软。
t_en: It never showed mercy to its enemies.

t_ch: 它生前最爱假扮成旁白。
t_en: Pretending to be the narrator was its favorite hobby.

t_ch: 请谨记凶手是杀毒软件。
t_en: Remember that the killer is the antivirus software.

t_ch: 完成了。人们都说为敌人落泪是高尚之举，但你并没有落泪。可能因为你还没那么高尚，也可能因为你们已经化敌为友。
t_en: It's done. Everyone says that it's noble to shed tears for your enemy, but you don't cry. Maybe it's because you're not that noble yet, or maybe your enemies have been turned into friends.

t_ch: 呃…这么两句就写完了？
t_en: Uh... two sentences and you're done writing?

t_ch: 你不是被查杀了吗？
t_en: Didn't you get eradicated?

t_ch: 一猜就是这样…
t_en: That's what I thought...

t_ch: 哈哈，逗你玩呢！我可是「超智能病毒」，哪有那么容易被查杀？
t_en: Haha, just kidding! I'm a Supersmart Virus, how can I be eradicated so easily?

t_ch: 唉，今天真是玩累了。我年纪也大了，从今以后金盆洗手，就在你电脑里养老吧。
t_en: I'm tired of playing today. I'm also getting old, so I'll be out of the game and retire in your computer from now on.

t_ch: 放心吧，我不仅不会给你添麻烦，还会帮助你保护你的电脑。绝对没有任何杀毒软件，比我「超智能病毒」更擅长消灭计算机病毒！
t_en: Trust me, not only will I not cause you any trouble, but I will help you protect your computer. There is absolutely no antivirus software that is better at killing viruses than me, the Supersmart Virus!

t_ch: 旁白：在仁慈与好奇心的双重作用下，你决定允许它留下。
t_en: Narration: With a combination of kindness and curiosity, you decide to allow it to stay.

t_ch: 够了，不要再假装旁白了！
t_en: Enough with pretending to be the narrator!

t_ch: 这是你的冰箱。理论上讲，里面应当装满了你的零食和饮料。
t_en: This is your refrigerator. Theoretically speaking, it should be full of your snacks and drinks.

t_ch: 但开拓者的直觉告诉你，现实情况似乎并非如此。
t_en: However, your Trailblazer instincts tell you that this is not the case...

t_ch: （打开冰箱）
t_en: (Open the fridge)

t_ch: 还是算了…
t_en: Forget it...

t_ch: 还是算了吧，眼不见为净……
t_en: Forget it. What you don't know can't hurt you...

t_ch: 果不其然，你的零食和饮料可怜巴巴地堆在一个狭小的角落，而几件你不认识的东西则登堂入室。
t_en: Just as you expected. Your poor snacks and drinks are mercilessly crushed into a corner, and the rest of the refrigerator is stacked to the brim with things you do not recognize.

t_ch: 一杯插着筷子的糊糊。半碗盖着保鲜膜的列车锅。一块翠缥色的布…等等，一块布？？？
t_en: A cup of goo with chopsticks sticking out of it. Half a bowl of Express Special covered in cling film. A piece of emerald green fabric... Wait, a piece of fabric???

t_ch: 看来你的冰箱很受欢迎嘛！
t_en: Looks like your refrigerator is popular!

t_ch: 怎么把我的冰箱都给塞满了？这都是谁放的东西？让我看看！
t_en: How could they stuff my refrigerator full like this? Who's been putting this stuff in here? Let me see!

t_ch: （检查冰箱）
t_en: (Inspect the refrigerator)

t_ch: 这都是些什么东西？你决定一探究竟……
t_en: What are these things? You decide to investigate...

t_ch: （看看插着筷子的糊糊）
t_en: (Look at the goo with chopsticks sticking out)

t_ch: 杯子上用清秀但略显幼稚的笔迹写道：三月七的自制冰淇淋ԅ(¯﹃¯ԅ)
t_en: There is elegant but slightly childish handwriting on the cup that reads: March 7th's homemade ice cream ԅ(¯﹃¯ԅ)

t_ch: 笨蛋，自制冰淇淋怎么放到冷藏室了？应该放到冷冻室才对吧。
t_en: Scatterbrain, why would you put home-made ice cream in the refrigerator section? It's supposed to go in the freezer, surely?

t_ch: （帮她转移到冷冻室）
t_en: (Move it into the freezer for her)

t_ch: 你将杯子放到了冷冻室中。希望成品能好吃。
t_en: You moved the cup into the freezer. Let's hope it tastes good when it's done!

t_ch: （全部喝掉）
t_en: (Drink it all)

t_ch: 你将未完成的「冰淇淋」一饮而尽。不得不说，还是蛮好喝的。
t_en: You down the unfinished "ice cream" in one gulp. You have to admit, it was delicious.

t_ch: （看看盖着保鲜膜的列车锅）
t_en: (Look at the Express Special covered in cling film)

t_ch: 列车锅上贴了一张便签：昨天晚餐没吃完，在你这里寄存一下。——列车长
t_en: There is a note posted on the Express Special: I couldn't finish this last night. I thought I'd store it here for a little while. — Conductor

t_ch: 为什么要寄存在我这里…厨房里难道没有冰箱吗？
t_en: Why do you have to store it here... Does the kitchen not have a refrigerator?

t_ch: （放在原处不动）
t_en: (Leave it where it is)

t_ch: 确实。既然是列车长寄存的东西，那还是别乱动了。
t_en: Probably for the best. If it belongs to the Conductor, it's best to just leave it alone.

t_ch: （全部吃掉）
t_en: (Eat it all)
